title: poa alpina subsp. xerophila
intro: Poa alpina subsp. xerophila, also known as alpine meadow grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass that grows in mountainous regions and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Poa alpina subsp. xerophila include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as potential uses in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
